result.full_summary: The gap between Canada's richest and poorest households grew in 2025, according to Statistics Canada. The income gap reached 46.7 percentage points, with the top 20% of households holding 65.7% of Canada's total net worth, averaging $3.5 million per household. In contrast, the bottom 40% held 3% of Canada's net worth, averaging $81,650 per household. The widening gap is attributed to slower wage growth and lower interest payments on savings for low-income households. Financial markets gained, but interest payouts declined and the job market softened, contributing to the increased gap. Insolvency practice MNP Ltd. noted that financial pressures remain uneven, with some Canadians struggling to cover bills and debt payments, while others are holding back on major financial decisions.
result.short_summary: Wealth gap between Canada's richest and poorest widened in 2025
result.title: Gap between richest and poorest Canadians kept widening in 2025, StatsCan says | CBC News

result.full_summary: Hootsuite founder Ryan Holmes has returned to the company as interim CEO, replacing Irina Novoselsky who stepped down after defending a contract with the US Department of Homeland Security. Novoselsky had led the company since 2023 and restored it to profitability. Holmes founded the company in 2008 and ran it until 2019. The company has been focusing on artificial intelligence tools and has been rebounding after facing profitability struggles. The change in leadership comes after controversy over the contract with the US Department of Homeland Security, which oversees Immigration and Customs Enforcement. Holmes plans to invest further in AI technology to help the company move faster and work smarter.
result.short_summary: Hootsuite founder Ryan Holmes returns as interim CEO
result.title: Hootsuite founder Ryan Holmes returns to CEO role on interim basis | CBC News

result.full_summary: The publishing industry is grappling with the rise of AI-generated content, with some authors using AI tools to assist with writing and others using them to generate entire books. A recent scandal involving a horror writer accused of using AI to write her novel has highlighted the need for guardrails to separate human-authored works from AI-generated ones. The industry is exploring ways to detect and label AI-generated content, with some self-publishing platforms asking authors to declare any AI-generated text. Literary agents are also seeing an uptick in 'undercooked' submissions, which they suspect may be AI-generated. The use of AI in publishing raises questions about the role of human creativity and the potential for copyright violations.
result.short_summary: Publishing industry navigates AI-generated content surge
result.title: How the publishing industry is navigating a surge of AI-generated content | CBC News

result.full_summary: Canada's economy added 14,000 jobs in March, recovering from the 84,000 job losses in February. The unemployment rate remained unchanged at 6.7%. Employment increased in the natural resources and other services industries, while declining in finance and insurance. Average hourly wages rose 4.7% to $37.73, the highest growth rate since October 2024. The Bank of Canada will closely monitor wage growth ahead of its next interest rate announcement on April 29. The modest job gain is seen as a positive sign, but not a strong indication of economic strength.
result.short_summary: Canada's economy adds 14,000 jobs in March after February's losses
result.title: Canada's economy adds 14,000 jobs in March | CBC News

result.full_summary: Air Canada is testing a new dispute resolution process to address passenger complaints. The airline will ask 500 randomly selected passengers to transfer their claims to a third-party arbitrator. The goal is to resolve customer grievances in a faster, efficient, and fair manner. The pilot project is funded by Air Canada and has been consulted with Transport Canada and the Canadian Transportation Agency. The test arbitration process will be run by a subsidiary of the UK-based CDRL Group, with decisions made within 90 days. The decision will be binding for Air Canada, but not for consumers, who can decline the results and proceed with the usual process. The pilot project aims to explore a solution that has yielded good results elsewhere and could potentially help clear the backlog of 95,000 complaints with the Canadian Transport Agency.
result.short_summary: Air Canada tests new program to resolve customer complaints
result.title: Air Canada is testing a new program to resolve customer complaints. Will it work? | CBC News

result.full_summary: LG Energy Solution, a South Korean battery maker, expects to post a first-quarter operating loss of 208 billion won due to weaker demand from electric vehicle makers. The company supplies batteries to major customers such as Tesla, General Motors, and Hyundai Motor. Revenue is expected to fall 2.5% to 6.6 trillion won from a year earlier. To offset the weakness in EV batteries, LGES is focusing on growing demand for energy storage systems driven by rising electricity needs for AI data centers. The company aims to triple its energy storage systems revenue this year and may benefit from a US House bill that could ban imports of certain Chinese-made energy storage systems.
result.short_summary: LG Energy Solution expects 1st quarter operating loss due to weak EV demand
result.title: NextStar’s South Korean parent company flags 1st quarter operating loss on weak EV demand | CBC News
